notes: E13-2 Print Actions (post-save auto-download, browser print, batch print) are hook/component infrastructure that will be wired into document pages in future epics (E14+). Those flows cannot be E2E tested until the consuming pages exist. E13-2 components are covered by unit and integration tests. This plan covers all E13-1 acceptance criteria and the E13-2 PrintSelectedButton component in isolation.

journeys.name: Navigate to Print Preferences Page
journeys.description: Verify the Print Preferences page is accessible from the sidebar navigation under the System section

steps.visual_check: App shell with sidebar visible, System section in navigation

steps.target: Print Preferences link in sidebar (Printer icon)

steps.expected_result: Print Preferences page loaded with page header

input_data.text: Configure how documents are handled after saving

steps.expected_result: Description text visible below page header
steps.visual_check: Print Preferences page with header, breadcrumbs (System > Print Preferences), description text, and preference table card. Purple-themed Concept D styling: #f4f2ff background, 12px radius card, custom shadow. Action bar shows disabled Save Preferences button and Reset to Defaults button.

journeys.name: Verify All 14 Document Types Listed
journeys.description: Confirm the preference table displays all 14 document types with correct human-readable labels and dropdown selectors

steps.visual_check: All 14 document types listed in the preference table, each with a Select dropdown in the My Preference column. Rows animate in with stepIn delay. Card has 12px radius and purple-tinted hover shadow.

steps.target: Select dropdown for Sales Invoice row

steps.visual_check: Select dropdown open showing three options: Auto-Download PDF, Browser Print Dialog, No Action. Dropdown styled with Concept D design.

journeys.name: Change User Print Preference and Save
journeys.description: Test changing a preference for a document type via the dropdown, verifying dirty state indicators, then saving and confirming persistence

steps.expected_result: Save Preferences button exists and is disabled (no changes yet)

steps.target: Select dropdown for Sales Invoice row in My Preference column

steps.expected_result: Sales Invoice preference changes to Auto-Download PDF

steps.target: unsaved changes warning in action bar
steps.expected_result: Amber warning indicator appears in action bar
steps.visual_check: Action bar shows amber warning triangle icon with 'You have unsaved changes' text. Save Preferences button is now enabled (purple #7c3aed). Sales Invoice row dropdown shows 'Auto-Download PDF'.

steps.target: Select dropdown for Purchase Order row in My Preference column

steps.expected_result: Purchase Order preference changes to Browser Print Dialog

steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Print preferences saved successfully' visible. Save Preferences button returns to disabled state. Unsaved changes warning disappears. Sales Invoice shows 'Auto-Download PDF', Purchase Order shows 'Browser Print Dialog'.

steps.expected_result: Navigate away from page (no blocker since changes saved)
Sales Invoice preference persisted as Auto-Download PDF after page reload
steps.visual_check: Print Preferences page reloaded with persisted values: Sales Invoice shows 'Auto-Download PDF', Purchase Order shows 'Browser Print Dialog'. Save button disabled (no changes).

journeys.name: Reset Preferences to Company Defaults
journeys.description: Test the Reset to Defaults flow including the confirmation dialog and verify preferences revert

steps.expected_result: Print Preferences page loads with current user preferences

steps.target: Reset to Defaults button (ghost style, in action bar)

input_data.text: Reset all print preferences to company defaults? This cannot be undone.

steps.visual_check: Confirmation dialog overlay visible with title 'Reset to Defaults', description warning about irreversibility. Cancel button and destructive-styled Reset to Defaults action button visible.

steps.target: Reset to Defaults action button (destructive style in dialog)

steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Preferences reset to defaults' visible. Preference table rows show source labels like '(company default)' or '(system default)' for all document types. Dropdown text is dimmed for inherited values.

journeys.name: Admin Views and Edits Company Defaults
journeys.description: Test that an admin user sees the Company Defaults section, can edit defaults, and save them. Verify the Company Default column appears in the user preferences table.
journeys.preconditions: Logged in as a user with ADMIN or SUPER_ADMIN role

steps.expected_result: Company Default column visible in user preferences table (admin sees 3 columns)

input_data.text: Set default print behaviour for all users in this company

steps.visual_check: Page shows two sections separated by a horizontal rule. Top section: user preferences table with 3 columns (Document Type, Company Default, My Preference). Bottom section: Company Defaults heading, description, Save Company Defaults button (disabled), and a table of all 14 document types with editable dropdowns.

steps.target: Select dropdown for Sales Invoice in Company Defaults table

steps.expected_result: Company default for Sales Invoice set to Auto-Download PDF

steps.target: Select dropdown for Purchase Order in Company Defaults table

steps.expected_result: Company default for Purchase Order set to Browser Print Dialog
Unsaved changes indicator visible next to Save Company Defaults button
steps.visual_check: Company Defaults section shows amber warning indicator and enabled Save Company Defaults button (purple). Sales Invoice shows Auto-Download PDF, Purchase Order shows Browser Print Dialog in the company defaults table.

Success toast 'Company print defaults saved successfully' visible. Save Company Defaults button returns to disabled state. In the upper user preferences table, Company Default column now shows 'Auto-Download PDF' for Sales Invoice and 'Browser Print Dialog' for Purchase Order.

journeys.name: Non-Admin Cannot See Company Defaults Section
journeys.description: Verify that a user with STAFF role does not see the Company Defaults section or the Company Default column
journeys.preconditions: Logged in as a user with STAFF role (not ADMIN or SUPER_ADMIN)

steps.expected_result: Page loads correctly for non-admin user

steps.target: preference table with 2 columns only (Document Type, My Preference)
steps.expected_result: Only 2 columns visible — no Company Default column

steps.target: absence of Company Defaults section heading
steps.expected_result: Company Defaults section is NOT rendered
steps.visual_check: Print Preferences page shows only the user preferences table with 2 columns (Document Type, My Preference). No separator, no Company Defaults heading, no Save Company Defaults button. Page ends after the preference table.

journeys.name: Preference Cascade with Source Labels
journeys.description: Verify that preferences show correct source labels: user-set values show normal text, company defaults show '(company default)' label, and fallback values show '(system default)'
journeys.preconditions: Admin has set company default for Sales Invoice to Auto-Download. User has NOT set a personal preference for Sales Invoice. User HAS set a personal preference for Purchase Order.

steps.expected_result: Print Preferences page loads with resolved preferences
Sales Invoice shows '(company default)' source label since user has no personal preference but company default exists

steps.target: Sales Invoice row dropdown — dimmed text style
steps.expected_result: Dropdown text for Sales Invoice is dimmed (text-muted-foreground) indicating inherited value

steps.target: Purchase Order row — no source label
steps.expected_result: Purchase Order shows no source label since user has set a personal preference

steps.target: Purchase Order row dropdown — normal text style
steps.expected_result: Dropdown text for Purchase Order is normal (text-foreground) indicating user-set value

steps.target: P60 row source label (no company default set)
steps.expected_result: P60 shows '(system default)' label — no user preference and no company default, so NONE fallback used
steps.visual_check: Preference table showing three states: Sales Invoice has '(company default)' label with dimmed dropdown, Purchase Order has user-set value with no label and normal text, P60 has '(system default)' label with dimmed 'No Action' dropdown.

journeys.name: Navigation Blocker for Unsaved Changes
journeys.description: Test that navigating away with unsaved changes shows a confirmation dialog, and the user can either stay or discard changes

steps.target: Select dropdown for Credit Note row

steps.expected_result: Credit Note preference changed — page now has unsaved changes
Dialog title warns about unsaved changes

input_data.text: Your changes will be lost if you leave this page without saving.

steps.visual_check: Navigation blocker dialog overlay visible with title 'You have unsaved changes', description about lost changes. Cancel button and destructive 'Discard & Leave' button visible.

steps.expected_result: Dialog closes, user stays on Print Preferences page
Still on Print Preferences page — navigation was cancelled

steps.target: Discard & Leave button in blocker dialog
steps.visual_check: Dashboard page loaded — user has navigated away from Print Preferences. Unsaved changes were discarded.

journeys.name: Company Default Changes Affect User Preference Resolution
journeys.description: Verify that when an admin changes a company default, users without personal preferences see the updated default
journeys.preconditions: Logged in as ADMIN. User has no personal preference for Delivery Note.

steps.expected_result: Print Preferences page loads with admin view

steps.target: Delivery Note row in user preferences (My Preference column)
steps.expected_result: Delivery Note currently resolves to No Action (no company default set)
Shows system default label — no company default exists

steps.target: Select dropdown for Delivery Note in Company Defaults table

steps.expected_result: Company default for Delivery Note set to Auto-Download PDF

steps.target: Delivery Note row in Company Default column
steps.expected_result: Company Default column shows Auto-Download PDF for Delivery Note

steps.target: Delivery Note row source label in My Preference section
steps.expected_result: Source label changed from '(system default)' to '(company default)' since company default now exists
steps.visual_check: Delivery Note row in user preferences table shows 'Auto-Download PDF' with '(company default)' label in dimmed text. Company Default column shows 'Auto-Download PDF'. Company Defaults section below shows Delivery Note set to Auto-Download PDF.

journeys.name: Loading Skeleton and Error State
journeys.description: Verify the page shows loading skeletons during data fetch and an error state with retry when the API fails

steps.target: skeleton loading placeholders (6 rows of Skeleton elements)
steps.expected_result: Loading skeletons visible while data is being fetched
steps.visual_check: Print Preferences page with skeleton loading state: 6 rows of animated skeleton rectangles (h-4 w-36 for labels, h-9 w-44 for dropdowns) shown in place of the preference table.

steps.expected_result: Skeletons replaced by actual preference table with 14 rows
steps.visual_check: Preference table fully loaded with all 14 document type rows, each with a Select dropdown. fadeInUp animation applied to page content.
